Wednesday, April 15, 2026 Daily Digest

Canva's $1B+ Agentic AI Bet, Claude Overtakes ChatGPT, MarTech's "Agentic" Shift

Today's top stories in AI + enterprise tech

1

Canva Acquires Simtheory + Ortto in Dual Agentic AI Play

Canva just made its biggest strategic move yet — acquiring both Simtheory (agentic AI platform) and Ortto (CDP + marketing automation) in what appears to be a $1B+ deal. The pitch: evolve from "design platform with AI tools" to "AI platform with design at its core." Simtheory's agents can autonomously pull and qualify Salesforce leads. Ortto brings unified customer data + multi-channel orchestration. Preview expected at Canva Create tomorrow.

Read on TechCrunch →
2

Claude Surpasses ChatGPT in App Store Downloads

For the first time ever, Anthropic's Claude briefly overtook ChatGPT as the top AI assistant app. The company that delayed its launch in 2023 "to avoid sparking an AI arms race" has quietly built its business on enterprise applications while OpenAI dominated consumer. Now the tables are turning — enterprise momentum is spilling into consumer mindshare.

Read on HumAI →
3

MarTech Cube: The Shift from Generative to Agentic AI

The 2026 martech landscape is defined by one transition: generative AI (content creation) → agentic AI (autonomous execution). The top 10 AI martech tools are now all about unified data, orchestrated workflows, and measurable ROI. The content flood era is ending; the execution era is beginning.

Read on MarTech Cube →
4

AI Agents for B2B: Orchestration Is What Actually Works

Colony Spark's deep dive on what's actually working in B2B AI marketing. The key insight: the fundamental shift in 2026 is orchestration — "AI agents that chain multiple tasks together, pass context between steps, and execute multi-stage workflows with minimal intervention." Standalone tools are out; connected workflows are in.

Read on Colony Spark →
5

Reality Check: Only 6% of B2B Marketers See Better Content Results

eMarketer drops a sobering stat: despite massive AI adoption for content creation, only 6% of B2B marketers say their content performance actually improved. AI reduces time on drafting and versioning, but productivity gains haven't translated to engagement or business results. The tool isn't the bottleneck — strategy and differentiation are.

Read on eMarketer →

💡 My Take

Read this one: The Canva acquisition story is the signal. They're not buying point solutions — they're assembling a full-stack AI marketing platform with autonomous agents at the center. When a $40B design company pivots this hard toward agentic AI + CDP + automation, it tells you exactly where enterprise marketing tooling is headed. Tomorrow's Canva Create event should be revealing.
